title: Find your event
surface: /albums — 263 event albums, sport / year filters, team-or-event search
summary: Discovery is event-first — start from the game you were in, not a 20K-photo firehose.

strategy.decision: Discovery is event-first: browse 263 albums filtered by sport and year, searchable by team or event. The album is the unit, not a flat photo stream.
strategy.why: The product model (decisions/0003) is discover → find → download/share, and Albums leads the IA (research/current-state/convergence-ledger.md §IA re-architecture). An athlete starts from "the game I played in."
strategy.shipped: Live at /albums (src/routes/albums): 263 albums, Latest-Photos sort, All-Sports / All-years filters, "Search team or event." Each card shows event, sport, photo count, and a quality-range badge.
strategy.gap: Discovery is strong; the bridge from "my event" → "my photos in it" (jersey self-search within an album) is the find step — see the find-my-photos page.
strategy.question: Do athletes arrive knowing their event/date, or do they need a "find me across all events" entry first? Needs the anchor-event walkthrough.

currentState.summary: 263 event albums; sport/year filters; team-or-event search; quality-range badges per card.
currentState.annotation: Sport is album-authoritative (albums.sport + the enforce_album_sport trigger). A new album needs its row before the #10 ingest runs, or its photos get sport_type=NULL.
